problems.title: Full nonlinear stability of subextremal Kerr
problems.fv_reason: Full nonlinear vacuum Einstein stability is a global PDE program; formalization should target lemmas and restricted regimes first.
problems.progress_summary: Partial progress exists in adjacent regimes; see references/TODO for precise theorem-level milestones.
problems.statement: Prove that asymptotically flat vacuum initial data sufficiently close to a Kerr slice with $|a|<M$ evolve to a spacetime with complete future null infinity, a regular event horizon, and quantitative convergence to a nearby Kerr metric modulo gauge and parameter modulation.
problems.math_required: Geometric analysis of Einstein vacuum equations; null structure equations; gauge fixing; weighted Sobolev spaces; vector-field and $r^p$ methods; trapping/superradiance control; modulation of mass and angular momentum.
problems.why_it_matters: This is the main Kerr stability conjecture in the physically relevant asymptotically flat vacuum setting.
problems.completion_criteria: A complete answer must specify the initial-data topology, prove global exterior existence, identify the final Kerr parameters, establish quantitative decay rates in a fixed gauge, and control all nonlinear error terms for the full subextremal range.
problems.implications: Would settle the core exterior black-hole stability problem for rotating vacuum black holes and give the exterior half of a precise cosmic censorship picture.
problems.notes: Partial: substantial adjacent results or special cases exist, but the statement as written is not fully settled. TODO(editorial): replace with a precise description of what is proved vs. conjectured.

problems.title: Uniform nonlinear stability as $a \to M^-$

problems.statement: Prove a version of Kerr stability whose constants and decay estimates remain uniform, or quantitatively controlled, as the spin approaches extremality.
problems.math_required: Degenerate redshift analysis; near-horizon scaling; semiclassical control near the photon region; uniform weighted energies in the surface-gravity parameter $\kappa$.
problems.why_it_matters: Near-extremal Kerr is the transition region between the standard subextremal theory and the qualitatively different extremal regime.
problems.completion_criteria: A complete answer must track dependence on $\varepsilon = 1 - |a|/M$ in all coercive estimates and separate unavoidable degeneracies from removable technical losses.
problems.implications: Would bridge ordinary Kerr stability to extremal dynamics and sharpen what actually fails as $\kappa \to 0$.
problems.notes: Open: no complete theorem matching the statement is currently recorded on this site. TODO(editorial): tighten if community consensus differs.

problems.implications: Clarifies how much of Kerr’s algebra persists under nonlinear perturbation.

problems.title: Charge–tail correspondence in the extremal limit
problems.fv_reason: Extremal limits often admit scaling symmetries and power-law expansions; coupled Maxwell tensor identities are partially algebraic, though tail–charge correspondence may still need PDE control.
problems.progress_summary: Near-extremal and extremal decay literature exists for several equations; a sharp unified “charge–tail” correspondence statement is not catalogued as proved on this site. See references/TODO.
problems.related_families_note: Kerr–Newman and near-extremal families differ from vacuum Kerr; asymptotic tail rates couple to charge and spin.
problems.statement: Relate leading-order late-time tails of coupled Maxwell or scalar fields on near-extremal Kerr–Newman backgrounds to conserved charges (electric/magnetic) and angular momentum in a quantitative theorem with explicit extremal limits.
problems.math_required: Einstein–Maxwell perturbations; Aretakis-type blow-up mechanisms; matched asymptotics; Price-law heuristics vs. theorem-level decay rates.
problems.why_it_matters: Extremal limits mix long-range electromagnetic and gravitational effects; tails encode observables in idealized models.
problems.completion_criteria: Theorems with explicit exponents and coefficients linking tail amplitudes to charge data, including convergence as extremality is approached.
problems.implications: Unifies extremal decay phenomenology with conserved-charge algebra.

problems.title: Stationary-axisymmetric Ernst reduction uniqueness domain for Kerr
problems.fv_reason: Ernst potential formulations reduce Einstein equations to elliptic PDE in 2D; uniqueness in fixed function classes is a natural formalization target.
problems.progress_summary: Classical reductions and uniqueness under strong hypotheses exist in the literature; the maximal sharp uniqueness domain for Kerr within the Ernst picture is not summarized as closed on this site. See references/TODO.
problems.statement: Characterize the largest natural function class (on the orbit space / rod structure) in which the Ernst reduction uniquely characterizes the Kerr solution among stationary axisymmetric vacuum spacetimes with given boundary/infinity data.
problems.math_required: Ernst equation; harmonic maps; boundary value problems for elliptic PDE; rod-structure formalism; inverse scattering techniques in 2+1 reductions where applicable.
problems.why_it_matters: The Ernst reduction is a workhorse for uniqueness; pinning down sharp domains feeds both rigidity and numerical reconstruction algorithms.
problems.completion_criteria: A theorem stating uniqueness (or non-uniqueness with explicit counterexamples) with hypotheses checkable without gauge ambiguity.
problems.implications: Strengthens stationary uniqueness cluster and informs inverse problems for multipole moments.
